markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar's second delay of GTA VI to November 19, 2026, has extended the window for major cultural and industry events, shaping trader focus on high-profile releases and announcements before launch. The added months for polish follow the shift from a May 2026 date, with marketing momentum expected to build in summer 2026, including a potential third trailer and pre-order opening around late June. This timeline amplifies interest in concurrent developments such as music album drops, tech milestones like new AI models, and geopolitical resolutions, as traders weigh historical patterns of delayed Rockstar titles against rapid shifts in entertainment news cycles. Upcoming earnings calls and confirmed marketing starts could further influence market-implied odds on specific outcomes.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Recent mistrials and split verdicts in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrials have driven trader consensus toward “No Prison Time” at 82%.** 

The latest development—a May 2026 mistrial after jurors deadlocked on the remaining Jessica Mann rape charge—followed a June 2025 split verdict (conviction on one count involving Miriam Haley, acquittal on another, mistrial on the third). Prosecutors have not yet confirmed plans for a fourth trial. Weinstein, now 74 and dealing with cancer and diabetes, remains incarcerated primarily due to his separate 2022 California conviction and 16-year sentence (under appeal), which runs consecutively. 

These repeated courtroom stalls, combined with his advanced age, documented health decline, and the existing California term, have lowered the perceived likelihood of substantial additional New York prison time. Markets reflect this uncertainty, with all multi-year buckets combined under 15%. Upcoming catalysts include any decision on retrying the Mann charge and rulings on Weinstein’s California appeal.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final between the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights sits tied 2-2, with Game 5 set for June 11 in Raleigh. Carolina's recent 5-3 road win in Game 4—powered by Jordan Staal's two goals and strong contributions from Nikolaj Ehlers—has shifted momentum toward the Hurricanes, aligning with their 57.5% implied probability. Multiple multi-goal lead changes across the series highlight both teams' offensive firepower and defensive vulnerabilities, while Carolina benefits from home-ice advantage and rest in the current spot. Vegas's ability to force overtime wins earlier keeps their 41.4% share competitive, though recent collapses underscore challenges in closing out games on the road. Trader consensus reflects these late-series dynamics and schedule positioning.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party and military through ongoing elite reshuffles and purges, with no credible internal challenges or succession moves emerging ahead of the 21st Party Congress. Recent diplomatic activity, including a June 2026 visit to North Korea, and policy emphasis on national security and technological self-reliance underscore his active leadership into 2026. Past rumors of decline or ouster have repeatedly failed to materialize, and analysts widely anticipate he will secure a fourth term at the late-2027 congress rather than exit earlier. Trader consensus at 92.3% for "No" reflects these structural barriers and the absence of any verifiable catalyst for removal before year-end.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 23% amid a fragmented field, driven by his national profile as California governor, fundraising strength, and visible role opposing the current administration through media appearances and policy clashes. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Kamala Harris, and Jon Ossoff follow in single digits, reflecting progressive mobilization, prior national exposure, and Senate visibility. Josh Shapiro and Pete Buttigieg trail further, supported by swing-state records and party infrastructure. With no formal announcements and the 2026 midterms approaching, early positioning via Southern outreach, book tours, and polling name recognition shapes probabilities, though consolidation behind any contender remains distant and subject to shifts from legislative outcomes or new candidacies.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 23% amid a fragmented field, driven by his national profile as California governor, fundraising strength, and visible role opposing the current administration through media appearances and policy clashes. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Kamala Harris, and Jon Ossoff follow in single digits, reflecting progressive mobilization, prior national exposure, and Senate visibility. Josh Shapiro and Pete Buttigieg trail further, supported by swing-state records and party infrastructure. With no formal announcements and the 2026 midterms approaching, early positioning via Southern outreach, book tours, and polling name recognition shapes probabilities, though consolidation behind any contender remains distant and subject to shifts from legislative outcomes or new candidacies.
